questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An isolated nucleus wich was initially at rest, disintegrates into two nuclei due to internal nuclear forces and no $\gamma$ rays are produced. If the ratio of their kinetic energy is found to be $\frac{64}{27}$ then
options: Ratio of their respective de Broglie wavelength is $\frac{\sqrt{64}}{\sqrt{27}}$
Ratio of their respective speed is $\frac{64}{37}$
Ratio of their respective nuclear radius is $\frac{3}{4}$
Ratio of their respective nuclear radius is $\frac{4}{3}$
questions.solution:
Momentum of each is equal and opposite.
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{m_1}{m_2}=\frac{27}{64} \\ & \frac{r_1}{r_2}=\frac{3}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A beam of light has two wavelengths $3100 Å$ and $4133 Å$ with a total intensity of $12.8 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^2$ equally distributed between the two wavelengths. The beam falls normally on an area of a clean metallic surface of work function 3.1 eV . Assume that there is no loss of energy by reflection and that each energetically capable photon ejects one electron. How many electrons will emit per second from the face area?
questions.solution:
$\lambda_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{hc}}{\phi}=\frac{12400}{3.1}=4000 Å$
so only $3100 Å$ will emit electrons. Then $\frac{\mathrm{I}}{2}=\mathrm{n}\left(\frac{\mathrm{hc}}{\lambda}\right) \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=\frac{12.8}{2}\left(\frac{\lambda}{\mathrm{hc}}\right)=10^{19}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a photoelectric effect experiment, photons of energy 5 eV are incident on the photo-cathode of work function 3 eV . For photon intensity $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}}=10^{15} \mathrm{~m}^{-2} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$, saturation current of $4.0 \mu \mathrm{~A}$ is obtained. Sketch the variation of photocurrent $i_p$ against the anode voltage $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{a}}$ for photon intensity $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}}$ (curve A ) and $I_B=2 \times 10^{15} \mathrm{~m}^{-2} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ (curve B) will be:
questions.solution:
Stopping potential, $\mathrm{V}=(\mathrm{h} v-\mathrm{W}) / \mathrm{e}=2 \mathrm{~V}$
In each case, the stopping potential is 2 V as energy of incident photon is the same. As the photon intensity is doubled, the saturation current is also doubled. The variation of $i_p$ against $V_a$ is sketched in the figure
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
